titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Investment Office, Yale University, Records Concerning Ethical Investments
Investment Office, Yale University, Records Concerning Ethical Investments
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 3, 2020 at 09:57

did.unittitle: Investment Office, Yale University, records concerning ethical investments
did.abstract: The records consist of reports and subject files of the Yale Investment Office concerning ethical investing, particulary in South Africa and tobacco companies.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 5-D (YRG 5-D), Investment and insurance records of Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is restricted. See Collection Contents for details.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Investment Office, Yale University, Records Concerning Ethical Investments (RU 296). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of reports and subject files of the Yale Investment Office concerning ethical investing, particulary in South Africa and tobacco companies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

corpname.part: Yale University. Office of Finance and Administration

did.unittitle: Records of investment policy for various responsibility issues primarily divestment in South Aftica
"Beyond Divestment: the Moral University": speech to AACHE
Public statements on South African investments
Letters to Richard C. Levin re: South Africa
Committee on the use of Hewitt Quadrangle, correspondence-re: Winnie Mandela City
Cornell University Research Report on South Africa
Cornell University Research Report on South Africa, second addendum
Corporation Committee of the whole meeting information packet on investor responsibility regarding tobacco
